rejection_reason: No single benchmark or benchmark pack can prove universal agency. The BenchCore pack is a governed, source-backed benchmark — not a universality proof.

claim_text: Agent_X can now build any agent safely
rejection_reason: The BenchCore pack covers 32 specific documents in a broadcast domain. It does not demonstrate capability across all domains or build safety guarantees.
This is a benchmark pack, not a full BenchCore implementation. 5 of 32 sources are deferred, 2 are optional, and no live system integration exists.

claim_text: Agent_X can now operate live MOS, OverDrive, Inception, customer databases, SSH log tailing, WSL, or production systems
rejection_reason: All 32 sources have live_dependency=false. Deferred boundaries document SSH, MySQL, WSL, and other live dependencies as explicitly out of scope.

claim_text: One project benchmark proves general intelligence or universal agency
rejection_reason: Benchmark packs measure specific, bounded capabilities. General intelligence and universal agency are not provable by any finite set of benchmarks.
Agent_X operates within benchmark constraints and governed policies. Autonomy is bounded by human review patterns, learning policies, and claim boundaries.

claim_text: Agent_X can self-evolve without human review
rejection_reason: The feedback loop and learning policies require governed, human-reviewed update processes. Self-evolution without review is explicitly prevented by policy.
